title: Cell Division (Mitosis)
description: Watch one cell become two through mitosis — the fundamental process of growth, repair, and reproduction.

narration.text: Each cell contains a complete copy of the organism's DNA. Mitosis duplicates this DNA and divides it into two identical cells.
A cell preparing to divide replicates all its chromosomes first, then aligns them at the cell's centre.
The chromosomes are pulled to opposite ends of the cell. A new membrane forms down the middle.
Division complete. Two genetically identical daughter cells, each with a full set of chromosomes.
This is how you grew from a single fertilised egg to 37 trillion cells — the same process, repeated billions of times.
When mitosis goes wrong and cells divide without stopping, the result is cancer. The process you're watching is normally tightly regulated.
